chef_comment: 5-course menu that changes every day, Danish chef, unique location.
Bistro with 100% local products, a lot of seafood and meat on the plate, and vintage natural wines, a must!
Hugo has worked in some beautiful establishments, Christophe Dufau among others, and has opened a cave à manger full of surprises.
For a very boozy lunch, the lunch menu changes every day, its set, there is no choice and that is what is good.
Niçoise cuisine, ravioli, daube, farcis and more (served by the fireplace in winter and in the garden with a petanque court in summer).

address: 20 Rue du Comté de Cessole, 06320 La Turbie
chef_comment: A pretty classic gastro which is very good value for money, a must!
To eat local chic.
The pho is to die for.
best_dish: Pho

chef_comment: Amatriciana + deep fried burrata, mama mia!!
best_dish: Amatriciana, Deep fried burrata

chef_comment: A must. The products and the precision, a journey! A wine list of the highest quality. A rare place, where Micka, Louis-Philippe, and Alexis amaze us.

address: 53 Av. des Frères Roustan, 06220 Golfe Juan (Le)
chef_comment: We come here for the fish. Super bouillabaisse. A cuisine committed to the sea and local catch. A real treat!
best_dish: Bouillabaisse

chef_comment: Pierrot's pirate lair. Beautiful wines, snacks to nibble on, and now a nice table open on the weekend with a menu.
Mathieu's delicious dishes and Franck's superb selection of wines make this a very appealing bistro!
The show on the plate, and in the room when Seb comes out of the kitchen. Beautiful local products on the menu, it's great!
Beautiful local produce and exciting cuisine from Thomas, who, assisted by Anaïs, delight us in one of the most beautiful valleys of the region. A small selection of wines that we like.
Hugo serves bomb small dishes, gourmet and tasty, a blast! And one of the few to offer exciting vintage wines. Top!
Nino's beautiful touch in the kitchen, Guigui as zébulon in the dining room, a bistro menu, and a lot of things to drink from a constantly expanding wine list.
Thomas's lively cuisine, tasteful, with produce that sometimes comes out of the adjoining garden. A great getaway!
You's great food, lively and fresh, wontons to die for, and Renaud's wine selection, which grows every day with sharp and highly drinkable wines.
best_dish: Wontons

chef_comment: Unmissable and timeless. A place to learn about natural wine! And Brice's cooking is full of audacity and subtlety, combining surf and turf! A chef's dream! And a top team led by Papa Olive!
Rome - Nice, beautiful pasta made in-house, generous and bright dishes, and the Italian viticulture we love. Heavenly!
best_dish: Pasta

chef_comment: A magical place, in a calm environment, the amazing touch of Hugo, creative and precise, and the energy of Luna, you feel good here.
Big "miams" on every bite of the menu concocted by Stephan, and Elise offers great wines to accompany them.

Beautiful selection of products, atypical and exciting wine list, with superb Slovenian finds, we go there for the gourmandize of the plates and the wonderful welcoming atmosphere, thanks to Simona and Léo.
Pierre's cooking is precise and delicious. He keeps with tradition but adds a bit of excitement. Barbajuans, capoun, pâtes au pistou...we indulge.
best_dish: Barbajuans, capoun, pâtes au pistou

chef_comment: The Pétard sisters, great wines on the table, an enchanting cave à manger, and dishes where the local products shine. Treat your tastebuds!
For Bruno's delicious and playful dishes and a fine selection of natural wines.
The sea all around and on the plate! At Romolo's, the beautiful gamberoni, brandacujun, fritto misto, you can enjoy Nadi's food with a view, and the drinks, orchestrated by Davide, are not forgotten!
best_dish: Gamberoni, brandacujun, fritto misto

chef_comment: The Izakaya of Nice. Lots of delicious dishes, superb gyoza, chirashi, a selection of good wine, and sakes to discover!
best_dish: Gyoza, chirashi

chef_comment: The location, in front of the sea, exciting cocktails, very nice selection of lively Mediterranean wines. And Francesca and her team's dishes that make your head spin. We want more.
For the fish couscous on Sundays.
Perfect bento lunch.
We don't go to a restaurant, we go Chez Etienne, an institution.

address: 72 Rue de la Paix Marcel Paul, 13006 Marseille

chef_comment: The best Chinese restaurant in Marseille.
best_dish: We love the pieds de cochons.

chef_comment: For the location, the atmosphere, and the modern primarily vegetable-centered cuisine.
In Antibes: fine wine selection, cuisine full of inspiration.
A restaurant open during the summer in the heart of the vineyards in Tavel. Great offal dishes.
The best Lebanese restaurant in Marseille.
You will be very well received here, the hospitality in this small restaurant is irreproachable, and its place in the Michelin guide is well-deserved.
best_dish: Don't miss: the calamars à la plancha accompagnés d'un coulis à l'orange, and the joue de bœuf.

chef_comment: The resistance is here! This mythical place in Nice proposes a beautiful menu around niçoise tradition, You shouldn't miss the pâtes au pistou and the pissaladière.
best_dish: Pâtes au pistou and pissaladière

chef_comment: Pop art decoration with an authentic feel. Le Bar des Oiseaux offers a very sophisticated and refined cuisine.
best_dish: The à la carte options are numerous and I would recommend you try the tartare au melon, the stracciatella fumée, the raviolis à l'encre de seiche, the calamars, or the mousse carbonara.

chef_comment: In a welcoming and family-like atmosphere, you will find the best fondue I have ever tasted! The brownie aux noisettes du Piémont is a must and everything is accompanied by a very nice selection of natural wines.
best_dish: Fondue and brownie aux noisettes du Piémont

chef_comment: Simple but delicate, this restaurant nestled in the narrow streets of Vieux Nice offers seasonal menus with fresh products from local producers.
Always received as if at home, the atmosphere is matched by well prepared dishes accompanied by a rich and varied selection of natural wines.
best_dish: Don't miss the vitello tonnato and the rougets en kadaïf.

chef_comment: You'll enjoy a delicious and generous cuisine, with plates to share in a very pleasant setting.
best_dish: I recommend the saucisse maison à la sauce barbecue, and the lentilles.

chef_comment: Always well received by the owner, Alex, this restaurant offers Italian cuisine the way we like it!
best_dish: The must: the real carbonara and for dessert, a good tiramisu, go there with your eyes closed!

chef_comment: The restaurant offers a generous cuisine with a focus on fresh seafood.
best_dish: My favourite is the ceviche à la saint jacques et au thon.

address: Rooftop de l'AC Hotel by Marriott Nice, Rue Honoré Sauvan, 59 Prom. des Anglais, 06000 Nice
chef_comment: We go there for the magnificent view but not only! You will find very good tapas, varied and full of flavour, with my favourite being the poulpe mijoté à la sauce tomate.
best_dish: Poulpe mijoté à la sauce tomate

chef_comment: Prestigious wine cellar and Spanish charcuterie.
Dynamic, organic natural wine cellar with a fine selection and low prices.
Innovative bar food and high-quality natural wines.
Charming restaurant, refined cuisine, and fine wines.
Rue Meyerbeer. Changing menu that's written on a chalkboard and great wines.
Rue Foresta. In a clearing with a view of the port. Small sharing plates, the best are the croquettes de parmesan.
best_dish: Croquettes de parmesan

chef_comment: All day dining on the boardwalk, natural wines, and small sharing plates.
Socca-based Nissart street food.
A wine bar with cheap sharing plates in the heart of Vieux Nice.

My restaurants offers a slightly updated version of Smørrebrød, working on enhancing flavor and hopefully offering something surprising. This is where I would come if I wanted classic Smørrebrød and I don't want to cook it myself, something like an easygoing piece of potato on rye with mayo and crispy onions. This is the classic lunch experience complete with harsh waiters that want to pour schnapps down your throat...it's part of the charm!.
best_dish: Very strong on the Herring, the fish dishes tend to be more interesting, it's elemental and has a balance of sweet and sour and things going on.

chef_comment: This is where I would come if I wanted classic Smørrebrød and I don't want to cook it myself, something like an easygoing piece of potato on rye with mayo and crispy onions. This is the classic lunch experience complete with harsh waiters that want to pour schnapps down your throat...it's part of the charm!.
best_dish: Very strong on the Herring, the fish dishes tend to be more interesting.

address: Gunnar Nu Hansens Pl. 9, 2100 Copenhagen, Denmark
chef_comment: A Mediterranean place with a huge grill. You can choose either shellfish or meat from the grill but the fish is normally what I would choose. I like the whole grilled fish with tossed salad, easygoing but sharp and good value for money. Open from Thursday to Saturday and always very full and noisy in a good way.

address: Hotel d'Angleterre, Kongens Nytorv 34, 1050 Copenhagen, Denmark
chef_comment: As a young chef, I worked in France and Italy so I'm a sucker for both cuisines. This is one of the greatest places that extravagantly showcases French cuisine. It's not cheap but it's all about lobster, turbot, and caviar so if you're up for having a treat then this is the place to go. Head chef Jakob De Neergaard is an expert at classic comfort flavors at a high level.

Michelin prices. Every year the Michelin guide comes out and every year we shake our heads that Mielcke & Hurtigkarl don't get a star. Asian-Japanese-nordic inspired with complex flavors that are controlled very well. Led by an Italian chef who is undogmatic and takes inspiration from all over the world. I find coming here inspirational because it's so different from other Copenhagen restaurants. Also one of Copenhagen's best sommeliers.

Local Italian restaurant owned by Italian Chef Fabbio Masson who has worked in Copenhagen for the past 30 years or more. He owns a few restaurants but this one is close to my home. The menu is set up so you get little antipasti, primi, secondo, and dolce, all in 6-7 servings. Classic easygoing Italian food with an ever-changing menu, always great antipasti and pasta, and very inexpensive (for Copenhagen). Probably one of the places I go to the most, it's good, simple, and close by.

A Korean restaurant not far from me by bicycle and it's also my business neighbor. Neither expensive nor inexpensive. Easygoing, extremely sharp comfort food. They have great kimchi, fantastic dishes from the barbecue, mussels, and langoustine. Indefinitely recommended. Excellent Korean food, great desserts, and one of the first of its kind in Copenhagen.

One chef doing everything, with a weekly changing menu. Home-style french cooking and only open four days a week. It's like going to eat at someone's house, everything is freshly made, no big mise en place, you cook in the afternoon and then open serve what you made a few hours before. The menu changes all the time so you could be getting a pithivier with morel sauce or sardines in escabeche.

Tinggaarden is about an hour from Copenhagen and is still owned and run by the same chef Jan Friis Mikkelsen and his family, as when I worked there as a young chef. It's based in an old farmhouse and he's been using local produce from the community since long before that was a thing. Very strong on local Danish sesonal ingredients, sourced very locally, the inspiration is with a slight tone of France, nothing is overdone, definitely not cheap but not super expensive either. Very down-to-earth and charming, it's an experience that will take you back in time.

The closest you get to Thai cooking in Copenhagen. Ranee the owner has been doing great Thai cooking for the past 15-20 years or so and she does an excellent job. There are dishes with strong chili that I like once in a while, it can be uncomfortable eating but in a good way! A lot of restaurants open and close in Copenhagen so I have deep respect that she's still here and I feel an obligation to visit and keep these places going.
best_dish: It's a small menu that changes often but there are very interesting entrees, green papaya salad, excellent larbs (great before the big curries), and an overall different and more authentic selection than other Thai places.
